questions.text: The people are the ________ of a country.
questions.explanation: People are the ultimate resource. Healthy, educated, and motivated people develop resources according to their requirements.

questions.text: A ________ is an official enumeration of population done periodically.
questions.explanation: In India, the first census was held in 1872, and the first complete census was taken in 1881.

questions.text: In India, a census is held every ________ years.
questions.explanation: Censuses are conducted every ten years.

questions.text: According to the 2011 Census, India's population was ________.
questions.explanation: India's population was 1,210.6 million (or 1.21 billion).

questions.text: Which state is the most populous state of India?
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh accounts for about 16% of the country's population.

questions.text: Which state has the lowest population in India?
questions.explanation: Sikkim has a population of just about 0.6 million (6 lakhs).

questions.text: Almost half of India’s population lives in just five states: UP, Maharashtra, Bihar, West Bengal and ________.
questions.explanation: These five states together house nearly half of the total population.

questions.text: Population density is calculated as the number of persons per ________.
questions.explanation: It provides a better measure of the distribution of people relative to land area.

questions.text: Which state has the highest population density in India as per 2011 census?
questions.explanation: Bihar has 1,102 persons per sq km.

questions.text: Which state has the lowest population density in India?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh has only 17 persons per sq km.

questions.text: The three main processes of change of population are birth rates, death rates and ________.
questions.explanation: The change in population is the result of births, deaths and migration.

questions.text: Birth rate is the number of live births per ________ persons in a year.
questions.explanation: It is a major component of population growth because in India, birth rates have always been higher than death rates.

questions.text: Death rate is the number of deaths per ________ persons in a year.
questions.explanation: The decline in death rates is the main cause of the rate of growth of the Indian population.

questions.text: Internal migration does not change the ________ of the population, but only the distribution.
questions.explanation: Internal migration happens within the country and doesn't affect the national total.

questions.text: In India, most migrations have been from ________ to ________ areas.
questions.explanation: This is due to the 'push' factor in rural areas (poverty) and 'pull' factor of cities (employment).

questions.text: The age composition of a population is divided into three groups: Children, Working Age, and ________.
questions.explanation: Children (below 15), Working Age (15-59), and Aged (above 59).

questions.text: Which age group is considered economically productive and biologically reproductive?
questions.explanation: This group comprises the working population.

questions.text: Sex ratio is defined as the number of females per ________ males.
questions.explanation: It is an important social indicator to measure the extent of equality between males and females.

questions.text: Which state in India has a sex ratio in favor of females (more than 1,000)?
questions.explanation: Kerala has a sex ratio of 1084 females per 1000 males (2011 census).

questions.text: A person aged 7 years and above, who can read and write with understanding in any language, is treated as:
questions.explanation: Literacy is a very important quality of a population.

questions.text: The distribution of the population according to different types of occupations is called:
questions.explanation: Occupations are classified as Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary.

questions.text: Primary activities include ________.
questions.explanation: Primary activities use natural resources directly.

questions.text: Tertiary activities include ________.
questions.explanation: Tertiary sector provides services.

questions.text: What is the most significant feature of the Indian population?
questions.explanation: India has a very large adolescent population (aged 10-19), which constitutes one-fifth of the total population.

questions.text: The National Population Policy (NPP) 2000 aims to provide free and compulsory school education up to age ________.
questions.explanation: Providing free and compulsory school education up to 14 years of age is a key objective of NPP 2000.

questions.text: Adolescents are generally grouped in the age group of ________ years.
questions.explanation: Adolescents are the future resource of the country.

questions.text: The difference between birth rate and death rate is called:
questions.explanation: Natural increase is the surplus of births over deaths.

questions.text: Large-scale migration from rural areas to cities has resulted in:
options: Increase in the size of population in cities
questions.explanation: Urbanization is a result of rural-to-urban migration.

questions.text: The dependency ratio includes ________ and ________ age groups.
questions.explanation: Children and the aged are considered dependents as they are not typically in the workforce.

questions.text: Population becomes 'human capital' when there is investment made in:
questions.explanation: Investment in human beings turns them into a productive asset.

questions.text: What does a high sex ratio in a state indicate?
questions.explanation: A higher number of females indicates better survival and social status of women.

questions.text: In developed countries, more people are engaged in ________ and ________ activities.
questions.explanation: Developed nations have higher workforce participation in industries and services.

questions.text: NPP 2000 also focused on ________.
questions.explanation: NPP 2000 is a comprehensive policy for population stabilization and welfare.

questions.text: What percentage of India's population is engaged in agriculture according to general estimates?
questions.explanation: About 64% of the population is engaged only in agriculture.

questions.text: Death rates have declined in India primarily due to:
options: Improvement in health and medical facilities
questions.explanation: Better healthcare and control of epidemics have reduced the death rate significantly.

questions.text: Literacy rates among males are ________ than among females in India.
questions.explanation: Historically, males have higher literacy rates due to social and economic factors.

questions.text: Which Union Territory has the lowest sex ratio as per 2011 Census?
questions.explanation: Daman and Diu had a sex ratio of 618.

questions.text: Which factor is responsible for the sparse population in the mountainous regions?
questions.explanation: Extreme terrain makes living conditions difficult, leading to lower density.

questions.text: Which plateau region in India is moderately populated?
questions.explanation: Hilly, dissected and rocky nature of the terrain, and moderate to low rainfall influence population density here.

questions.text: The magnitude of population growth refers to:
options: The total population of an area
The number of persons added each year
The rate at which population increases
questions.explanation: It is the absolute increase in numbers.
